root_text.ud2.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā uruvelāyaṁ viharati najjā nerañjarāya tīre mucalindamūle paṭhamābhisambuddho.
root_text.ud2.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sattāhaṁ ekapallaṅkena nisinno hoti vimuttisukhapaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.ud2.1:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena mahā akālamegho udapādi sattāhavaddalikā sītavātaduddinī.
root_text.ud2.1:2.2: Atha kho mucalindo nāgarājā sakabhavanā nikkhamitvā bhagavato kāyaṁ sattakkhattuṁ bhogehi parikkhipitvā uparimuddhani mahantaṁ phaṇaṁ vihacca aṭṭhāsi:
root_text.ud2.1:2.3: “mā bhagavantaṁ sītaṁ, mā bhagavantaṁ uṇhaṁ, mā bhagavantaṁ ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphasso”ti.
root_text.ud2.1:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassa sattāhassa accayena tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhāsi.
root_text.ud2.1:3.2: Atha kho mucalindo nāgarājā viddhaṁ vigatavalāhakaṁ devaṁ viditvā bhagavato kāyā bhoge viniveṭhetvā sakavaṇṇaṁ paṭisaṁharitvā māṇavakavaṇṇaṁ abhinimminitvā bhagavato purato aṭṭhāsi pañjaliko bhagavantaṁ namassamāno.
root_text.ud2.1:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.1:1.2: At one time, when he was first awakened, the Buddha was staying in Uruvelā at the root of the Mucalinda tree on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.ud2.1:1.3: There the Buddha sat cross-legged for seven days without moving, experiencing the bliss of freedom.
translation_text.ud2.1:2.1: Just then a large storm blew up out of season, bringing seven days of precipitation, with cold winds and overcast skies.
translation_text.ud2.1:2.2: Mucalinda, the dragon king, came out from his abode, encircled the Buddha’s body with seven coils and spread his large hood over his head, thinking,
translation_text.ud2.1:2.3: “May the Buddha not be hot or cold, nor be bothered by flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, or reptiles.”
translation_text.ud2.1:3.1: When seven days had passed, the Buddha emerged from that state of immersion.
translation_text.ud2.1:3.2: When he knew the heavens were clear and cloudless, Mucalinda unwrapped his coils from the Buddha’s body. Hiding his own form, he manifested in the form of a brahmin youth. He stood in front of the Buddha, venerating him with joined palms.
translation_text.ud2.1:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.1:5.2: who see the teaching they have learned.
translation_text.ud2.1:5.3: Kindness for the world is happiness
translation_text.ud2.1:5.4: for one who’d not harm a living creature.
translation_text.ud2.1:6.1: Dispassion for the world is happiness
translation_text.ud2.1:6.2: for one who has gone beyond sensual pleasures.

variant_text.ud2.1:1.2: mucalindamūle → muccalindamūle (sya-all) | ti → … siriṁsapa … (bj, sya-all, km, pts-vp-pli1)

reference_text.ud2.1:1.1: bj24.148, dr25.117, mc0.105, ms18Ud_71, msdiv11, ndp20.73, pts-vp-pli10
